summary: Refactored negative-item ingest in src/lib/credit-analysis.ts to stop using the combined=>all-bureaus fallback. Added src/lib/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.ts to resolve negative-item presence from derogatory parser evidence first, then linked account bureau evidence via existing getAccountPresence, then single-bureau report context, and otherwise leave bureau presence false/omitted. Added focused combined-report tests covering single-bureau evidence, linked-account evidence, and no-evidence behavior. Verification passed: `npm run test -- src/lib/__tests__/credit-analysis-account-presence.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npx eslint src/lib/credit-analysis.ts src/lib/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.ts src/lib/__tests__/credit-analysis-account-presence.test.ts src/lib/__tests__/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.test.ts`.

findings:
- Changed files: src/lib/credit-analysis.ts; src/lib/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.ts; src/lib/__tests__/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.test.ts
- Tests added/updated: src/lib/__tests__/credit-negative-item-bureau-presence.test.ts
- Remaining P1.1 work: other Phase 1.1 slices outside this ingest change were not touched; likely remaining work is broader parser/consumer alignment beyond negative-item combined-report presence.
- Risk: negative-item fallback now prefers omission/false when no bureau evidence exists, so any downstream code that previously depended on guessed all-bureau presence may surface fewer bureaus until more parser/linkage evidence is available.
- Risk: linked-account reuse still depends on current creditor/account-number matching heuristics in credit-analysis.ts; mismatches will intentionally yield no inferred bureau presence.
